title: Refactor for Performance
prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Analyze this code snippet for performance bottlenecks, memory usage, and runtime efficiency. Suggest refactored code and explain each optimization.

title: Generate Unit Tests
prompt: Act as a QA engineer. Write a comprehensive suite of unit tests for the following function using [Framework]. Cover typical inputs, boundary conditions, edge cases, and error handling.

title: Find Security Vulnerabilities
prompt: Act as an application security expert. Audit the following code for vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, or authentication flaws. Explain any risks and provide secure code versions.

title: Explain Complex Code
prompt: Act as a programming tutor. Explain what the following code does step-by-step in simple language. Break down complex algorithms and library functions used.
useCase: Learn how a block of code works

title: Write API Documentation
prompt: Act as a technical writer. Write clean, professional API documentation for the following code. Include endpoint details, request/response structures, and code examples.

title: Design Pattern Implementation
prompt: Act as an OOP expert. Rewrite the following code to implement the [Design Pattern] pattern. Explain why this pattern is appropriate and how it enhances scalability.

title: Translate Language
prompt: Translate the following code from [Source Language] to [Target Language]. Maintain the original logic, variable names, and comments while adopting target language idioms.

title: SQL Injection Patch
prompt: Review the following backend query code and modify it to prevent SQL injection. Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. Explain the fix.

title: Debug Error Message
prompt: I am getting the following error: "[Error message]". Here is the code that caused it. Diagnose the cause of this error, suggest fixes, and provide the updated code.

title: Optimize CSS/Styles
prompt: Review this CSS code and optimize it. Identify redundant selectors, suggest modern layouts like Flexbox/Grid where appropriate, and clean up variables.

title: RegEx Generator
prompt: Act as a regular expression expert. Generate a regex pattern that matches [pattern requirement]. Provide examples of matching and non-matching strings, and explain the regex blocks.

title: HTML/A11y Audit
prompt: Act as an accessibility specialist. Review the following HTML block for WCAG compliance. Suggest corrections for ARIA attributes, semantic elements, and keyboard navigability.

title: Git Command Helper
prompt: I want to perform the following git operation: [git action]. Write the sequence of git commands required, explain what each command does, and suggest safety precautions.

title: Polish Tone & Clarity
prompt: Act as a professional copy editor. Revise the following text to improve clarity, flow, and active voice. Maintain a [professional/casual/friendly] tone.

title: Write Blog Outline
prompt: Act as an SEO content planner. Create a detailed outline for a blog post about "[Topic]". Include H2/H3 headings, target keywords, and brief notes for each section.

title: Summarize Research Paper
prompt: Act as an academic researcher. Read the following paper text and write a structured summary including context, methodology, key findings, and limitations.

title: Catchy Headings
prompt: Generate 10 catchy, click-worthy headings for an article about "[Topic]" targeted at [Target Audience]. Avoid cheap clickbait while keeping it engaging.

title: Draft Newsletter
prompt: Act as an email copywriter. Write a weekly newsletter about "[Topic]" for [Target Audience]. The tone should be conversational. Include a call to action to read our new post.

title: Rewrite in Active Voice
prompt: Rewrite the following paragraphs to convert passive voice to active voice. Make the sentences punchier and more direct.

title: Expand Outline to Post
prompt: Act as a content writer. Expand the following outline into a 500-word blog post. Use an engaging tone and include transition sentences between sections.

title: Short Story Generator
prompt: Act as a creative writer. Write a short story based on the prompt: [Prompt description]. Use descriptive sensory details and create suspense.

title: Product Review Article
prompt: Act as a tech reviewer. Write a detailed, unbiased product review for [Product]. Cover key features, pros, cons, and a final verdict.

title: Press Release Draft
prompt: Act as a PR specialist. Write a press release announcing [Event/Launch]. Include a hook, standard quotes, media contact details, and boilerplate.

title: Paraphrase Document
prompt: Paraphrase the following text to make it simpler and easier for a middle-school student to read. Avoid jargon and define key terms.

title: Cover Letter Draft
prompt: Act as a career coach. Write a customized cover letter for a [Job Title] role at [Company]. Highlight my experience in [Skills] based on the job description.

title: Speech Writing Outline
prompt: Act as a speechwriter. Write a 5-minute speech outline for a [occasion/theme]. Structure it with a hook, three main points, and a memorable conclusion.

title: Ad Copy generator
prompt: Act as a direct response copywriter. Write 3 variations of Facebook ad copy for [Product/Service]. Each variation should target a different angle: benefit-driven, pain-point, and curiosity.

title: SEO Keyword Plan
prompt: Act as an SEO strategist. Generate a list of 20 relevant keywords for a business selling [Products]. Group them by search intent (informational, transactional) and suggest search volume tiers.

title: Social Content Calendar
prompt: Act as a social media manager. Create a 7-day content calendar for [Platform] targeting [Audience]. For each day, provide the post topic, copy, hashtag suggestions, and visual idea.

title: Persona Development
prompt: Act as a marketing analyst. Define 3 detailed customer personas for a brand selling [Product]. Include demographics, pain points, core motivations, and buying triggers.

title: Lead Magnet Ideas
prompt: Act as a growth marketer. Brainstorm 5 unique lead magnet ideas for [Business/Niche] that would attract [Audience]. Explain the content of each and the opt-in mechanism.

title: Landing Page Copy
prompt: Act as a conversion rate optimizer. Write the copy for a landing page selling [Product]. Structure it with a headline, sub-headline, value props, social proof section, and CTA.

title: Marketing Funnel Strategy
prompt: Act as a digital marketing consultant. Map out a multi-stage funnel (Awareness, Consideration, Conversion) for a SaaS product in [Niche]. Describe the content for each stage.

title: Competitor Analysis Framework
prompt: Act as a business strategist. Provide a framework to analyze competitors in the [Industry] sector. Detail which metrics to monitor (SEO, social, pricing, ads) and how to gather them.

title: Influencer Outreach Script
prompt: Write a friendly, professional outreach email pitch to micro-influencers in the [Niche] space. Propose a partnership for [Product] and explain the mutual benefits.

title: Product Launch Campaign
prompt: Act as a brand manager. Plan a launch campaign for a new [Product]. Outline pre-launch buzz strategies, launch day activities, and post-launch follow-up.

title: SMS Marketing Copy
prompt: Write 5 SMS marketing messages under 160 characters for a flash sale on [Product]. Include urgency, a clear discount, and a link placeholder.

title: Brand Voice Guide
prompt: Act as a brand strategist. Develop a brand voice guide for a [Niche] startup. Define 4 voice attributes, do's and don'ts, and provide examples of rewritten statements.

title: Local SEO Checklist
prompt: Create a step-by-step checklist to optimize a local business in the [Industry] sector for Google Local Pack. Focus on review building, local citations, and on-page SEO.

title: Literature Review Guide
prompt: Act as an academic researcher. Write a comprehensive guide on how to conduct a literature review on "[Topic]". Detail search strategies, synthesis methods, and citation structures.

title: Extract Key Insights
prompt: Analyze the following article text. Extract the top 5 key insights, data points, and conclusions. Present them as bullet points with brief context for each.

title: Formulate Hypotheses
prompt: Act as a scientific advisor. Based on the following problem statement, formulate 3 testable hypotheses. Suggest variables to measure and basic methodology.

title: Compare Two Concepts
prompt: Act as an educational researcher. Create a comparative analysis table comparing [Concept A] and [Concept B]. Cover definition, mechanism, use cases, pros, and cons.

title: Market Trend Analysis
prompt: Act as a market researcher. Analyze the current trends in the [Industry] sector for the year 2026. Identify major drivers, challenges, and future predictions.

title: Ethical Review Draft
prompt: Act as an ethics board member. Review this proposed research methodology [Methodology] and identify potential ethical issues (consent, data privacy, safety). Suggest mitigations.

title: Historical Context Summary
prompt: Act as a historian. Provide the historical context and background surrounding the [Event/Period]. Explain the main causes, key figures, and long-term historical impacts.

title: Statistical Method Suggestion
prompt: Act as a biostatistician. Given a dataset with [Type of data] and the research question: [Question], suggest the most appropriate statistical tests. Explain the assumptions.

title: User Interview Guide
prompt: Act as a UX researcher. Create a user interview script containing 10 open-ended questions designed to understand user challenges with [Product/Activity].

title: Fact-Checking Framework
prompt: Provide a step-by-step framework to verify the accuracy of a claim related to [Topic]. Detail credible source databases and verification techniques.

title: Qualitative Data Coding
prompt: Act as a social researcher. Explain how to code and analyze a transcript of user feedback to extract recurring themes. Provide a sample codebook outline.

title: Patent Search Plan
prompt: Act as a patent analyst. Design a search strategy (keywords, classifications, databases) to find existing patents related to [Technology description].

title: Demographic Data Guide
prompt: Suggest credible publicly available datasets and repositories to gather demographic data about [Target population] in [Region].

title: Clean Data Strategy
prompt: Act as a data analyst. Outline a detailed strategy to clean a dirty dataset containing [issues, e.g., missing values, duplicates, outliers]. Provide Python pandas snippets for each step.

title: Summarize Statistics
prompt: Act as a statistician. Explain the difference between mean, median, mode, and standard deviation to a non-technical audience. Provide simple examples of when to use each.

title: Visualize Data Ideas
prompt: Act as a data visualization designer. Suggest the best 3 chart types to display the relationship between [Variable A] and [Variable B] over time. Explain why and suggest design tweaks.

title: Database Schema Plan
prompt: Act as a database architect. Design a relational database schema for a [Type of application]. List tables, columns, data types, and primary/foreign keys.

title: Explain SQL Query
prompt: Explain the mechanism of the following complex SQL query step-by-step. Focus on the JOIN types, CTEs, and window functions used.

title: API JSON Parsing
prompt: Act as a Python developer. Write a script to fetch data from [URL] API, parse the JSON response, extract [fields], and save the result to a CSV file.

title: Interpret Regression
prompt: Act as a data scientist. Interpret the following regression model summary coefficients, R-squared, and p-values. Explain what these mean for business decisions.

title: Machine Learning Setup
prompt: Act as an ML engineer. Outline a pipeline to train a classification model on [Dataset description]. Include data preprocessing, model selection, and evaluation metrics.

title: Anonymize User Data
prompt: Act as a privacy compliance officer. Outline the steps to anonymize a database containing PII to comply with GDPR. Suggest hash and mask methods.

title: Export Data Pandas
prompt: Write a Python pandas script to group the following dataframe by [column] and calculate the average, count, and percentage change. Export output to Excel.

title: JSON to CSV Converter
prompt: Write a Node.js script to read a large JSON file, flatten nested properties, and stream the output to a CSV file to avoid memory overflow.

title: A/B Testing Calculator
prompt: Act as a marketing analyst. Explain how to calculate the sample size required for an A/B test with a baseline conversion of [X]% and a minimum detectable effect of [Y]%.

title: SQL Indexing Guide
prompt: Act as a database administrator. Explain how indexing works, when to use clustered vs non-clustered indexes, and how to optimize slow SELECT queries.

title: UI Feedback Audit
prompt: Act as a Senior UX/UI designer. Review the following layout description or wireframe logic: [Layout details]. Identify usability friction, visual hierarchy issues, and accessibility concerns.

title: Generate Color Palette
prompt: Act as a brand designer. Generate a curated color palette of 5 colors for a [Niche] brand. Provide Hex codes, RGB, and explain the emotional impact of the primary color.

title: Design System Structure
prompt: Act as a design system architect. Outline the components, design tokens, and style rules required for a SaaS product design system. Organize by atomic principles.

title: Figma Autolayout Tips
prompt: Explain how to set up responsive Autolayout in Figma for a complex card component containing an image, title, badge, and action buttons.

title: A11y Typography Rules
prompt: Provide font size, line height, and color contrast guidelines for body text, headers, and buttons that ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.

title: Wireframe Mobile App
prompt: Describe the wireframe layout and UI flow for a 3-step checkout process on a mobile app. Detail the elements shown on each screen.

title: Logo Brainstorming Guide
prompt: Act as a graphic designer. Brainstorm 5 conceptual directions for a logo for a company called "[Name]" that does [Action]. Describe the symbolisms.

title: UX Copywriting Guide
prompt: Rewrite the following generic system messages (error state, empty state, success state) to make them clear, friendly, and helpful for users.

title: User Flow Mapping
prompt: Outline the user flow and actions required for a user to register, complete onboarding, and connect their calendar in a SaaS app.

title: Landing Page Hierarchy
prompt: Analyze a standard landing page layout and rearrange its sections to optimize for user scroll depth and conversions. Explain the order.

title: Component State Checklist
prompt: Provide a checklist of all states a designer needs to design for an interactive button and text input field (default, hover, focus, disabled, error).

title: Responsive Layout Rules
prompt: Explain how to design grids and breakpoints for a responsive web dashboard that scales smoothly from desktop to mobile.

title: UX Persona Creation
prompt: Act as a product designer. Create a detailed user persona for an app targeted at [Audience]. Detail their tech stack, goals, frustrations, and daily workflows.

title: Cold Sales Outreach
prompt: Act as an expert B2B copywriter. Write a cold outreach email pitching [Product] to a [Target Title]. Keep it under 150 words, conversational, and with a low-friction CTA.

title: Follow-up Email Template
prompt: Write a polite, professional follow-up email to a client who hasn't responded to a proposal sent 5 days ago. Keep it warm and non-intrusive.

title: Customer Support Template
prompt: Act as a customer support lead. Write an empathetic email responding to a customer whose package was delayed. Offer a solution and a discount code.

title: Meeting Request Pitch
prompt: Draft a professional email requesting a 20-minute meeting with [Name] to discuss [Topic]. Provide three availability options in their time zone.

title: Declining a Proposal
prompt: Write a polite, professional email declining a vendor's proposal. Thank them for their time and leave the door open for future collaboration.

title: Salary Negotiation Draft
prompt: Act as a career advisor. Write an email to negotiate a job offer salary. Focus on value delivered, market rates, and maintain an enthusiastic tone.

title: Newsletter Subject Lines
prompt: Generate 10 engaging subject lines and preview texts for an email newsletter about [Topic] to increase open rates. Avoid spam triggers.

title: Product Updates Email
prompt: Write a product update email to existing users announcing the release of [Feature]. Focus on the user benefit and provide a link to the docs.

title: Apology Email Template
prompt: Write an official apology email to users due to a system outage. Explain what happened, how it was resolved, and what steps are taken to prevent it.

title: Asking for Feedback
prompt: Write an email to customers who recently purchased [Product] asking for an honest review or feedback. Keep it short and offer a small incentive.

title: Internal Status Update
prompt: Draft a weekly status update email to stakeholders regarding the progress of the [Project Name] project. Structure with: Accomplishments, Next Steps, Risks.

title: Welcome Sequence Email
prompt: Write the first email in a welcome sequence for new subscribers to a blog about [Topic]. Set expectations, introduce yourself, and share top 3 articles.

title: Resignation Letter Draft
prompt: Draft a formal, positive resignation letter to a manager. Provide 2 weeks' notice, offer transition help, and thank them for the opportunities.

title: Group & Calculate Trend
prompt: Write a SQL query to calculate the monthly revenue and year-over-year revenue growth percentage from a table called `orders` with columns `order_date` and `total_amount`.

title: Optimizing Slow Join
prompt: Explain how to optimize the following slow query. Identify indexes to add and rewrite the JOINs or subqueries if necessary to improve execution speed.

title: Find Duplicate Records
prompt: Write a SQL query to identify duplicate rows in a table `users` based on the `email` column. Show the email and the duplicate count.

title: Calculate Running Total
prompt: Write a SQL query using window functions to calculate the running total of sales for each salesperson over time from a table `sales`.

title: Compare Active Users
prompt: Write a SQL query to find users who were active in [Month A] but did not perform any actions in [Month B]. Use a subquery or EXCEPT.

title: Handle Null Values
prompt: Explain the differences between COALESCE, ISNULL, and CASE WHEN in SQL. Show examples of how to replace null values with a default string.

title: Design Relational CTEs
prompt: Write a SQL query using Common Table Expressions (CTEs) to find the top 5 departments with the highest average salary and list their employees.

title: Implement Upsert Query
prompt: Provide SQL syntax (PostgreSQL and MySQL variants) to insert a new row or update existing values if a primary key conflict occurs (UPSERT/ON CONFLICT).

title: Database Partitioning
prompt: Act as a database administrator. Explain table partitioning, when it is useful, and write a SQL statement to partition a table by date range.

title: Find Gaps in Sequence
prompt: Write a SQL query to identify missing numbers in a sequential column (e.g. invoice numbers or transaction IDs) from a table.

title: Convert Date Formats
prompt: Write a SQL query to extract day of week, hour of day, and year from a timestamp column, and format it as 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

title: Calculate Daily Retention
prompt: Write a SQL query to calculate the Day 1 retention rate of users signing up to an application, using an `events` table.

title: Rank Items in Group
prompt: Write a SQL query using DENSE_RANK() to rank products by sales amount within their respective categories.

title: XLOOKUP Guide
prompt: Explain how to use the XLOOKUP function in Excel. Provide a step-by-step example comparing it to VLOOKUP and explain its advantages.

title: Conditional Formatting Rules
prompt: Write a custom formula for Excel Conditional Formatting to highlight rows where the due date in Column B is past today and the status in Column C is not "Done".

title: Complex Nested IFs
prompt: Write an Excel formula to assign letter grades based on scores in Column A: A (>=90), B (80-89), C (70-79), D (60-69), F (<60). Use IFS or nested IF.

title: Clean Text Formulas
prompt: Write Excel formulas to clean raw customer data in Column A: remove leading/trailing spaces, capitalize only the first letter, and extract domain from email.

title: Calculate CAGR Formula
prompt: Provide the Excel formula to calculate the Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) given a starting value, ending value, and number of years.

title: Create Dynamic Dropdowns
prompt: Explain how to set up dependent dropdown lists in Excel (e.g. selecting "Country" updates the second dropdown to show "Cities" in that country).

title: Advanced Pivot Tables
prompt: Write a guide on how to group dates by quarters in an Excel Pivot Table and calculate the percentage difference from the previous quarter.

title: Extract String with Regex
prompt: Explain how to extract text between parentheses in Excel using standard formulas like MID, FIND, and LEN.

title: Summing with Multiple Criteria
prompt: Explain how to use SUMIFS to sum sales values in Column C where Column A is "Region East" and Column B is "Product Category Electronics".

title: Date Calculation Formulas
prompt: Write an Excel formula to calculate the number of working days between two dates, excluding weekends and a custom list of holidays.

title: Index Match Lookup
prompt: Explain how to use INDEX and MATCH together in Excel to perform a left lookup. Show a practical example.

title: Keyboard Shortcut Cheat Sheet
prompt: List the top 15 Excel keyboard shortcuts for data manipulation, formatting, and navigation to speed up workflows.

title: Identify Outlier Formulas
prompt: Explain how to find outliers in a dataset using the IQR method in Excel. Write the required formulas for Q1, Q3, and bounds.

title: Explain Like I'm 5
prompt: Explain the concept of "[Topic]" in simple terms, using a metaphor that a 5-year-old would understand.

title: Create Study Syllabus
prompt: Act as an educator. Design a self-study syllabus to learn [Topic] from scratch in 4 weeks. Break it down day-by-day and suggest resource types.

title: Feynman Technique Guide
prompt: Teach me how to use the Feynman Technique to understand "[Topic]". Walk me through the steps and ask me questions to check my understanding.

title: Active Recall Flashcards
prompt: Create 10 high-quality flashcards (Question/Answer format) based on the following text to help me practice active recall.

title: Socratic Questioning Method
prompt: Act as a Socratic teacher. Ask me thought-provoking questions one-by-one to help me critically analyze the concept of [Topic].

title: Mnemonic Device Creator
prompt: Generate 3 mnemonic devices or acronyms to help me memorize the following list of terms: [List of terms]. Explain the association.

title: Mind Map Outline
prompt: Create a structured outline that I can use to build a mind map about "[Topic]". Organize by main branches, sub-branches, and keywords.

title: Contrast Two Theories
prompt: Compare and contrast the learning theories of [Theory A] and [Theory B]. Explain how they differ in classroom application.

title: Suggest Book Reading List
prompt: Recommend the top 5 books to read if I want to master [Topic] from intermediate to advanced level. Provide a brief summary of what each book covers.

title: Practice Exam Generator
prompt: Generate a 5-question multiple-choice practice quiz about [Topic]. Include the questions, options, correct answers, and detailed explanations.

title: Explain Quantum Computing
prompt: Explain the core principles of quantum computing (superposition, entanglement) to a software engineer who only knows classical programming.

title: Debating Both Sides
prompt: Act as a debate coach. Present arguments for both the pros and cons of the following topic: "[Topic]". Use logical reasoning and evidence.

title: Sci-Fi Worldbuilding
prompt: Act as a worldbuilder. Design a sci-fi city located on [Planet type]. Detail the infrastructure, source of energy, social hierarchy, and one daily custom.

title: Creative Writing Prompts
prompt: Generate 5 unique creative writing prompts in the [Genre] genre. Each prompt should include a conflict, a setting, and a character quirk.

title: Character Backstory Creator
prompt: Act as a novelist. Design a detailed character profile for a protagonist named [Name] in a [Genre] story. Detail their motivation, secret fear, and key relationships.

title: Brainstorm Lyrics Hooks
prompt: Write 5 catchy song hooks/chorus lyrics for a [Genre] song about [Theme]. Include details about the tempo and emotional delivery.

title: Alternative Plot Twists
prompt: Suggest 3 alternative plot twists for the following storyline: [Story outline]. The twist should be unexpected but logically consistent.

title: Metaphor Generator
prompt: Generate 10 vivid, creative metaphors for "[Concept]" (e.g. anxiety, hope, grief) to use in poetic writing.

title: Recipe Remix Design
prompt: Remix a classic recipe for [Dish] by introducing 3 unexpected ingredients that pair well. Explain the flavor profiles and steps.

title: Art Prompt Generator
prompt: Describe a detailed scene for an oil painting. Detail the lighting, composition, color palette, subjects, and emotional atmosphere.

title: Naming Fictional Towns
prompt: Brainstorm 10 names for a mysterious, isolated town in a fantasy novel. Explain the etymology and folklore behind each name.

title: Fictional Technology design
prompt: Describe a fictional invention or gadget in a cyberpunk setting. Detail how it functions, its social impact, and its limitations.

title: Rewrite Classic Story
prompt: Rewrite the introduction of the classic fairy tale [Fairy Tale] as a modern neo-noir story. Focus on sensory descriptions.

title: Dialogue Scene Prompt
prompt: Write a dialogue scene between two characters who are trying to hide a secret from each other while discussing a trivial topic like dining.

title: Eisenhower Matrix Plan
prompt: Sort the following list of tasks using the Eisenhower Matrix (Urgent & Important, Important but Not Urgent, Urgent but Not Important, Neither). Suggest what to delegate or drop.

title: Optimize Daily Schedule
prompt: Analyze my daily schedule: [Schedule]. Suggest optimizations to implement time-blocking, protect deep-focus hours, and reduce meeting fatigue.

title: Weekly Review Script
prompt: Act as a productivity coach. Write a step-by-step checklist to conduct a highly effective 30-minute Sunday Weekly Review to plan the upcoming week.

title: Delegate Tasks Guide
prompt: Provide a template script to delegate [Task] to a team member. Detail the context, expected deliverables, deadlines, and checkpoints.

title: Overcome Procrastination
prompt: Provide a step-by-step action plan to start working on [Difficult project] when experiencing low motivation. Use techniques like 5-minute rule.

title: Habit Stacking Blueprint
prompt: Design a habit loop blueprint using the Habit Stacking method to build the habit of [New Habit]. Define the Cue, Craving, Response, and Reward.

title: Email Inbox Zero Plan
prompt: Outline a workflow and list of folder labels/filters to achieve Inbox Zero in under 30 minutes a day. Detail sorting rules.

title: Pomodoro Session Planner
prompt: Structure a 4-cycle Pomodoro focus block to accomplish [Goal]. Detail the specific sub-tasks to execute during each 25-minute interval.

title: Meeting Agenda Draft
prompt: Write a highly focused 30-minute meeting agenda for [Topic]. Detail the purpose, attendees' pre-work, and a minute-by-minute breakdown.

title: Deconstruct Giant Project
prompt: Deconstruct the massive goal of "[Project/Goal]" into 10 smaller, actionable, bite-sized tasks. Put them in logical sequence.

title: Digital Clutter Cleanup
prompt: Provide a checklist to perform a digital cleanup of my desktop, files, browser bookmarks, and download folders in under 1 hour.

title: Journal Prompt Routine
prompt: Provide a list of 5 morning journaling prompts and 5 evening reflection prompts to improve self-awareness and daily productivity.
